results.core_alignment_check: Same obligation: processor engaging a sub-processor only with controller authorization/approval.
results.condition_check: Compatible: contract requires controller approval before engaging a sub-processor, matching prior specific or general written authorization.
results.modality_check: Compatible: 'shall not ... unless approved' is restrictive and meets the regulatory prohibition with authorization condition.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: same target/action and prior-authorization timing; no weaker constraint shown.
results.contract_source_text: The Processor shall not engage a Sub-processor, unless this is approved by the Controller by

results.core_alignment_check: Yes: both clauses require the processor to inform the controller about intended changes involving the addition or replacement of sub-processors under a general written authorization.
results.condition_check: Compatible: both are triggered in the case of general written authorization; the contract adds objection mechanics and acceptance timing, which do not weaken the notice duty.
results.modality_check: Compatible: the contract uses 'shall inform,' matching the regulatory mandatory obligation.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: the subject matter, recipient, and timing/content of the notice are the same; the added 30-day objection rule concerns a separate consequence and does not contradict the notice requirement.
results.contract_source_text: In the case of general written authorisation, the Processor shall inform the Controller of any intended changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors, thereby giving the Controller the opportunity to object to such changes. However, the Controller cannot object to any intended changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors, if the new Sub-processor provides sufficient guarantees with respect to implementation of appropriate technical and organizational measures in such a manner that the Processing will meet the requirements of the GDPR as outlined in this Agreement. If the Controller does not object to changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors within 30 days from the Processor’s notification of the intended changes, such changes shall be deemed to be accepted by the Controller.

results.core_alignment_check: processor/controller/notify intended changes concerning addition or replacement of sub-processors: same obligation
results.condition_check: same condition: in case of general written authorization
results.modality_check: equal or stronger: shall inform
results.other_constraints_check: same target/content and timing; contract adds controller objection mechanics and 30-day acceptance, which are additional and not weaker
results.contract_source_text: In the case of general written authorisation, the Processor shall inform the Controller of any intended changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors, thereby giving the Controller the opportunity to object to such changes. However, the Controller cannot object to any intended changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors, if the new Sub-processor provides sufficient guarantees with respect to implementation of appropriate technical and organizational measures in such a manner that the Processing will meet the requirements of the GDPR as outlined in this Agreement. If the Controller does not object to changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors within 30 days from the Processor’s notification of the intended changes, such changes shall be deemed to be accepted by the Controller.

results.core_alignment_check: Processor/controller liability for sub-processor obligations: same real-world obligation, with contract matching the processor remaining liable to the controller for sub-processor performance.
results.condition_check: No special condition in either text; compatible.
results.modality_check: Regulation uses shall; contract uses shall, equal force.
results.other_constraints_check: Same target and scope; 'its sub-processor's obligations' is substantively equivalent to 'sub-processor's obligations'.
results.contract_source_text: Processor shall remain liable to the Controller for the performance of its Sub- processor's obligations.

results.core_alignment_check: Same actor (processor) and same obligation: ensure authorized persons processing personal data are bound by confidentiality or statutory confidentiality duty.
results.condition_check: No additional triggering condition in either text; compatible.
results.modality_check: Regulation is mandatory and contract states the processor ensures this, which is equally mandatory.
results.other_constraints_check: Same target group and same confidentiality requirement; no looser timing/manner/location constraints.
results.contract_source_text: Processor ensures that persons authorised to process the Personal Data have committed themselves to confidentiality or are under an appropriate statutory obligation of confidentiality.

results.core_alignment_check: Processor obligation to inform the Controller of a legal requirement before processing, when processing is required by Union/Member State law and disclosure is not prohibited on important public-interest grounds, matches the regulation's same processor-to-controller notification duty.
results.condition_check: Compatible: both are triggered when the processor is subject to a legal requirement to process, with the carve-out that informing the controller is not required if law prohibits such information on public-interest grounds.
results.modality_check: Compatible: the contract uses 'shall inform', which is mandatory and at least as strong as the regulation's 'shall inform'.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: 'before Processing' matches 'before processing'; the public-interest prohibition qualifier is substantively the same.
results.contract_source_text: to Process Personal Data only in accordance with the instructions from the Controller, cf. clause 2 of this Agreement, including with regard to transfers of Personal Data to a Third Country or an international organisation, unless required to do so by Union or Member State law to which the Processor is subject; in such a case, the Processor shall inform the Controller of that legal requirement before Processing, unless that law prohibits such information on important grounds of public interest;

results.core_alignment_check: Yes: the contract sub-item and parent clause address one of the same Art. 32(1) security measures—restoring availability and access to personal data in a timely manner after a physical or technical incident—matching the regulation's item (c).
results.condition_check: Compatible: both are triggered by a physical or technical incident and require timely restoration of availability/access.
results.modality_check: Compatible: the parent clause uses 'shall implement appropriate technical and organisational measures' and the sub-item states an included measure, which is at least as strong as the regulation's requirement.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: the timing ('in a timely manner') and object ('Personal Data') align with the regulation; no weaker location/target constraints appear.
results.contract_source_text: the ability to restore the availability and access to Personal Data in a timely manner in the event of a physical or technical incident;

results.core_alignment_check: Yes. The regulation requires the processor to implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure security appropriate to risk; the contract clause likewise requires the Controller and the Processor to implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure a level of security appropriate to the risk, including examples such as encryption and confidentiality/integrity/availability/resilience.
results.condition_check: Compatible. Both are conditioned on taking into account the state of the art, costs, nature/scope/context/purposes of processing, and the risk to rights and freedoms of natural persons.
results.modality_check: Compatible. The contract uses 'shall implement', which is mandatory and at least as strong as the regulation's obligation.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible. The contract includes equivalent security-target and risk-based qualifiers, and its listed measures are additional specifics rather than weaker constraints.
results.contract_source_text: Data relating to criminal convictions and offences (cf. GDPR art. 10). *Sensitive data includes: data revealing racial or ethnic origin, political opinions, religious and/or philosophical beliefs, trade union membership, processing of genetic data or biometric data for the purpose of uniquely identifying a natural person, data concerning health and/or data concerning a natural person's sex life or sexual orientation Please note that for the processing of sensitive data, explicit consent of the Data Subject is required. Appendix 2 Security of Processing This Appendix forms part of the Agreement. 1. Taking into account the state of the art, the costs of implementation and the nature, scope, context and purposes of Processing as well as the risk of varying likelihood and severity for the rights and freedoms of natural persons, the Controller and the Processor shall implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure a level of security appropriate to the risk, including inter alia as appropriate: (a) encryption of Personal Data when transmitted via public networks and in connection with remote access to Controller’s systems; (b) the ability to ensure the ongoing confidentiality, integrity, availability and resilience of Processing systems and Services;

results.core_alignment_check: Yes. Both clauses impose on the processor a duty to implement appropriate technical and organisational measures for security/protection of personal data and data subjects' rights.
results.condition_check: Compatible. The regulation ties the measures to risk level; the contract requires measures sufficient to meet GDPR and protect rights, which is at least as protective and not weaker.
results.modality_check: Compatible. 'Agrees to implement' is an обязательство equivalent in force to 'shall implement'.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible. The contract's broader reference to GDPR compliance and protection of data subjects' rights covers the security objective; it is not looser than the regulation's risk-based security requirement.
results.contract_source_text: The Processor agrees to implement appropriate technical and organisational measures in such a manner that the Processing of the Personal Data will meet the requirements of the GDPR and other applicable national and EU data protection law and ensure the protection of the rights of the Data Subjects.

results.core_alignment_check: Processor obligation to allow for and contribute to audits/inspections matches the regulation's same audit/inspection assistance duty; recipient/auditor is narrower (external qualified auditor mandated by Controller) but still within 'controller or another auditor mandated by the controller'.
results.condition_check: Compatible: audits are conducted by an auditor mandated by the Controller, which fits the regulation's condition.
results.modality_check: Mandatory obligation ('to allow for and contribute') meets the regulatory 'shall'.
results.other_constraints_check: 'During normal business hours' and 'reasonably necessary' are compatible/stricter limitations; confidentiality condition is an added constraint and does not weaken the duty.
results.contract_source_text: to allow for and contribute during normal business hours to reasonably necessary audits including inspections, conducted by an external qualified auditor mandated by the Controller, solely for the purpose of fulfilment of the Controller's obligations laid down in Article 28 of the GDPR and for accurately stipulated research questions in this connection provided that such external qualified auditor is subject to and bound by confidentiality obligations as stipulated in clause 12 of this Agreement;

results.core_alignment_check: Processor obligation to assist the Controller with DPIA-related compliance, including carrying out a DPIA and prior consultation where required, matches the regulation's processor assistance duty for DPIA compliance.
results.condition_check: Compatible: both are triggered by the DPIA context; the contract adds 'where necessary' and 'taking into account the nature of the Processing,' which are reasonable qualifiers and not weaker.
results.modality_check: Compliant: 'shall assist' is mandatory and matches the regulatory obligation.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: the contract covers assistance in compliance with DPIA obligations and prior consultation of supervisory authorities where required, which is coextensive with or broader than the regulation's requirement.
results.contract_source_text: where necessary and taking into account the nature of the Processing, to assist the Controller in its compliance with an obligation to carry out a data protection impact assessment (“DPIA”) and prior consulting of supervisory authorities where required, cf. articles 35 and 36 of the GDPR;

results.core_alignment_check: Yes: both address the processor's obligation to regularly test, assess, and evaluate the effectiveness of technical and organisational security measures for processing.
results.condition_check: Compatible: the contract states a process for testing, assessing and evaluating effectiveness, which matches the regulation's regular testing/assessment/evaluation requirement.
results.modality_check: Equal or stronger: the contract uses mandatory language ('shall' / stated process) rather than mere permission.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: the contract covers the same security-measures effectiveness duty for processing; no weaker timing, target, or location constraint appears.
results.contract_source_text: a process for testing, assessing and evaluating the effectiveness of technical and organisational measures for ensuring the security of the Processing. 2. In assessing the appropriate level of security account shall be taken in particular of the risks that are presented by Processing, in particular from accidental or unlawful destruction, loss, alteration, unauthorised disclosure of, or access to Personal Data transmitted, stored or otherwise processed. 3. The Processor hosts the data, including Personal Data, of the Controller via Microsoft Cloud Azure. The online hosting Services are delivered from data centres solely situated within EU, in Ireland and the Netherlands. 4. Microsoft Azure platform provides the security measures as described on www.Processor.com. 5. Particular security measures to be taken out by the Processor under this Agreement are specified in detail on www.Processor.com (Processor's Security and Privacy Protection Policies). results.core_alignment_check: Yes. Both provisions impose the processor's duty to ensure that persons under its authority with access to personal data process those data only on the controller's instructions, with the same legal-law exception.
results.condition_check: Compatible. The contract adds the exception for processing required under applicable EU or national law, which matches the regulation's exception.
results.modality_check: Compatible. 'Must ensure' is mandatory and at least as strong as 'shall ensure'.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible. The target group, access to personal data, and instruction-only limitation are substantively the same; the contract's wording is equivalent and not looser.
results.contract_source_text: The Processor must ensure that the persons performing work for the Processor and who have access to Personal Data, only process such Personal Data as instructed by the Controller, unless processing is required under applicable EU law or national legislation. 13. Liability

results.core_alignment_check: Same obligation in substance: processor/persons acting under its authority may access/process personal data only on controller instructions; contract says processor shall process personal data only on documented instructions from the controller, with the agreement constituting those instructions.
results.condition_check: Compatible: both are conditioned on controller instructions; contract is at least as specific by requiring documented instructions.
results.modality_check: Equal or stronger: 'shall ... only' is restrictive and meets the regulatory obligation.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: 'persons acting under its authority who has access to personal data' is covered by the processor's processing/access under the agreement; no weaker timing/location/manner constraint.
results.contract_source_text: The Controller hereby authorises the Processor to Process the Personal Data on behalf of the Controller on the terms and conditions set out in this Agreement. The Processor shall Process the Personal Data only on documented instructions from the Controller. The Parties agree that this Agreement shall constitute the instructions as of the date of the Agreement. The Processor may – unless otherwise specifically is set out in the Agreement – apply all relevant means, including software, servers and IT systems.

results.core_alignment_check: Processor assists Controller with prior consultation of supervisory authorities; contract expressly includes assistance with prior consulting of supervisory authorities where required, matching the same processor assistance obligation.
results.condition_check: Regulation applies where processing would result in high risk absent mitigation measures; contract applies where required in connection with DPIA and prior consulting of supervisory authorities, which is substantially the same triggering situation.
results.modality_check: 'shall assist' is matched by an obligation to assist; not merely permission.
results.other_constraints_check: Contract adds 'where necessary' and 'taking into account the nature of the Processing,' which are compatible and not weaker than the regulation's condition.
results.contract_source_text: where necessary and taking into account the nature of the Processing, to assist the Controller in its compliance with an obligation to carry out a data protection impact assessment (“DPIA”) and prior consulting of supervisory authorities where required, cf. articles 35 and 36 of the GDPR;

results.core_alignment_check: Yes. The regulation requires that persons acting under the processor's authority with access to personal data process them only on the controller's instructions. The contract requires the processor to process personal data only in accordance with the controller's instructions, which is the same substantive instruction-only limitation and covers processing by persons under the processor's authority through the processor's compliance obligation.
results.condition_check: Compatible. Both apply to processing of personal data under the controller's instructions, with the contract adding a lawful-processing exception where Union or Member State law requires otherwise, which is consistent with GDPR.
results.modality_check: Compatible. The contract uses a mandatory 'shall' obligation and a restrictive 'only in accordance with' formulation, which is at least as strong as the regulation's 'shall ensure'.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible. The contract's scope is processing personal data, including transfers, and the timing/exception structure does not weaken the instruction-only requirement.
results.contract_source_text: to Process Personal Data only in accordance with the instructions from the Controller, cf. clause 2 of this Agreement, including with regard to transfers of Personal Data to a Third Country or an international organisation, unless required to do so by Union or Member State law to which the Processor is subject; in such a case, the Processor shall inform the Controller of that legal requirement before Processing, unless that law prohibits such information on important grounds of public interest;

results.condition_check: Compatible: both apply to the breach notification content; the contract's 'where possible' qualifier is not materially inconsistent with the requirement for inclusion of the listed items, but it is incomplete.
results.modality_check: Weaker/incomplete: the regulation requires the notification to at least include multiple specified elements, while the contract only requires description of one element.
results.other_constraints_check: No conflicting timing/location/manner constraints beyond the incomplete scope; the issue is omission of required content.
results.contract_source_text: The aforementioned notification shall describe the nature of the Personal Data Breach including where possible:

results.core_alignment_check: No: the regulation requires the processor to notify the controller of a personal data breach; the contract instead requires the controller to notify the processor of possible unauthorized use of login information/passwords/credentials or other security breaches related to the Main Agreement.
results.condition_check: Different trigger and subject matter: personal data breach vs possible unauthorized use of credentials or other security breaches related to the agreement.
results.modality_check: Both are mandatory, but the contract obligation is on the controller rather than the processor.
results.other_constraints_check: Timing is stricter ('immediately' vs 'without undue delay'), but the recipient/actor/object mismatch is substantive.
results.contract_source_text: The Controller shall immediately notify the Processor in writing after becoming aware of any possible unauthorised use of log-in information, passwords, credentials or other security breaches related to the Main Agreement. 7. Records of Processing activities

results.core_alignment_check: Processor obligation to process personal data only in accordance with controller instructions matches the regulation's requirement to process personal data on documented instructions from the controller.

results.modality_check: Equal or stronger: 'shall process only in accordance with' is mandatory and restrictive, satisfying 'shall process on documented instructions'.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: the contract is at least as strict, expressly covering transfers to third countries/international organisations and the legal-requirement exception.
results.contract_source_text: to Process Personal Data only in accordance with the instructions from the Controller, cf. clause 2 of this Agreement, including with regard to transfers of Personal Data to a Third Country or an international organisation, unless required to do so by Union or Member State law to which the Processor is subject; in such a case, the Processor shall inform the Controller of that legal requirement before Processing, unless that law prohibits such information on important grounds of public interest;

results.core_alignment_check: Yes. The regulation prohibits processor transfers of personal data to a third country or international organization unless the controller has given prior specific or general authorization. The contract says the processor must process personal data only in accordance with the controller's instructions, including with regard to transfers to a third country or international organisation, which substantively requires controller authorization/instructions before such transfers.
results.condition_check: Compatible. Both are triggered by transfers of personal data to a third country or international organization; the contract's carve-out for Union/Member State law is an additional lawful-transfer condition and does not weaken the controller-authorization requirement.
results.modality_check: Compatible. The regulation's prohibition without prior authorization is met by the contract's restrictive 'only in accordance with the instructions from the Controller' framing, which is at least as strong.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible. The contract is not looser on timing or scope; it covers the same transfer context and is stricter by tying transfers to controller instructions, with a separate legal-obligation exception that does not undermine the baseline rule.

results.core_alignment_check: The regulation requires the processor to inform the controller immediately when an instruction infringes GDPR or other data protection provisions. The contract requires the processor to inform the controller before processing if the processor is required by Union or Member State law to process in a way that would otherwise conflict with the controller's instructions, but it does not cover informing the controller that an instruction itself infringes GDPR/data protection law.
results.condition_check: Different trigger: regulation is triggered by an instruction infringing GDPR or other data protection provisions; contract is triggered by a legal requirement to process despite instructions.
results.modality_check: Both use mandatory language ('shall inform'), so modality is not weaker.
results.other_constraints_check: Timing differs ('immediately' vs 'before Processing'), but the main issue is the different trigger and subject matter; the contract is not the same obligation.

shared_groups: Data Return Deletion And Post-Termination Handling
results.core_alignment_check: Processor assistance to delete and/or return the Controller’s data including Personal Data overlaps with the processor’s duty to return or delete personal data to the controller after services end, but this clause is framed as assistance on request and includes export/expense conditions rather than a direct processor obligation.
results.condition_check: Regulation triggers after the end of the provision of services; contract triggers during and upon expiry of the Agreement and only if Controller requests assistance. This is narrower/different and does not fully cover the automatic post-service duty.
results.modality_check: The contract uses 'if Controller requests' and 'shall be rendered' for assistance, which is mandatory only upon request; it does not impose the processor’s standalone obligation to return/delete.
results.other_constraints_check: The regulation requires return or deletion after end of services; the contract adds 'including export', 'during and upon expiry', and payment conditions in Appendix 4, which are additional and not equivalent to the required unconditional post-termination return/delete duty.
results.contract_source_text: If Controller requests the Processor’s assistance to delete and/or return (i.e. export) the Controller’s data including the Personal Data during and upon the expiry of the Agreement such assistance shall be rendered by Processor at Controller’s expense and is charged under the conditions set out in Appendix 4. 10. Sub-processing

results.core_alignment_check: processor obligation to process personal data only in accordance with controller instructions matches the requirement to process personal data on documented instructions from the controller
results.condition_check: compatible: both are limited to processing under controller instructions, with the contract adding a lawful-compulsion exception and notice duty
results.modality_check: equal-or-greater force: 'shall process only in accordance with' is mandatory and at least as strict as 'shall process on documented instructions'
results.other_constraints_check: compatible: the contract is stricter by expressly covering transfers to third countries/international organisations and requiring notice if law compels contrary processing
results.contract_source_text: to Process Personal Data only in accordance with the instructions from the Controller, cf. clause 2 of this Agreement, including with regard to transfers of Personal Data to a Third Country or an international organisation, unless required to do so by Union or Member State law to which the Processor is subject; in such a case, the Processor shall inform the Controller of that legal requirement before Processing, unless that law prohibits such information on important grounds of public interest;

shared_groups: Personal Data Breach Notification And Content
results.core_alignment_check: Partial overlap: both concern the notification of a personal data breach and its content, but the contract only covers the nature of the breach and does not include the other required items (DPO contact details, consequences, mitigation measures).
results.condition_check: Compatible: both apply to the breach notification content; the contract adds a qualifier 'where possible' but does not conflict with the trigger.
results.modality_check: Compatible on the covered item: 'shall describe' is mandatory and at least as strong as the regulation's inclusion requirement.
results.other_constraints_check: Looser/incomplete on content scope: the regulation requires at least four categories of information, while the contract only states one category and leaves the rest out.
results.contract_source_text: The aforementioned notification shall describe the nature of the Personal Data Breach including where possible:

shared_groups: Personal Data Breach Notification And Content
results.core_alignment_check: The contract addresses the breach notification content requirement only in part: it requires the notification to describe the nature of the personal data breach, which matches item (a), but it does not include the other required items (DPO name/contact details, consequences, and mitigation measures).
results.condition_check: No special triggering condition beyond the notification itself; compatible as far as it goes.
results.modality_check: The contract uses 'shall', which is mandatory and at least as strong as the regulation's requirement.
results.other_constraints_check: The contract is narrower on content: it covers only the nature of the breach and omits the remaining mandatory notification elements.
results.contract_source_text: The aforementioned notification shall describe the nature of the Personal Data Breach including where possible:

shared_groups: Personal Data Breach Notification And Content
results.core_alignment_check: Yes: the regulation requires the breach notification to include one of its listed contents, namely the consequences of the breach; the contract sub-item states 'the likely consequences of the Personal Data Breach,' which is the same content requirement and is in-line with the parent clause requiring the notification to describe the nature of the breach including, where possible, such details.
results.condition_check: Compatible: the regulation requires inclusion in the notification; the contract uses a descriptive sub-item within that notification context, with no conflicting condition.
results.modality_check: Compatible: the parent clause is mandatory ('shall describe'), so the sub-item content is mandatory as part of that obligation.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: 'likely consequences' is substantively equivalent to 'consequences of the breach' and is not weaker; it is a direct content match rather than a pointer elsewhere.
results.contract_source_text: the likely consequences of the Personal Data Breach;

results.core_alignment_check: Processor must inform Controller of intended changes to addition/replacement of sub-processors; same actor/action/object/recipient as regulation.
results.condition_check: Both apply in the case of general written authorization.
results.modality_check: Contract uses 'shall inform', equal-or-stronger than regulatory obligation.
results.other_constraints_check: Same subject matter and timing/trigger; added objection/acceptance mechanics do not weaken the notice duty.
results.contract_source_text: In the case of general written authorisation, the Processor shall inform the Controller of any intended changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors, thereby giving the Controller the opportunity to object to such changes. However, the Controller cannot object to any intended changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors, if the new Sub-processor provides sufficient guarantees with respect to implementation of appropriate technical and organizational measures in such a manner that the Processing will meet the requirements of the GDPR as outlined in this Agreement. If the Controller does not object to changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors within 30 days from the Processor’s notification of the intended changes, such changes shall be deemed to be accepted by the Controller.

results.core_alignment_check: No. The regulation requires the processor to assist the controller in consulting the supervisory authority before processing in a high-risk DPIA-style situation; the contract instead requires cooperation with the supervisory authority in performing its tasks on the controller’s request. Same general subject (supervisory authority cooperation) but different obligation and recipient/aim.
results.condition_check: Different trigger: regulation applies where processing would result in high risk absent mitigation measures and is about prior consultation; contract is only on the Controller’s request.
results.modality_check: The contract uses a mandatory cooperation duty, but it is not the same assistance obligation.
results.other_constraints_check: Different target and timing: consultation prior to processing in high-risk cases versus cooperation with the authority in performance of tasks, without the specific prior-consultation/high-risk constraint.
results.contract_source_text: to cooperate (including representatives of Processor), on the Controller’s request, with the supervisory authority in the performance of its tasks;

results.core_alignment_check: Yes. Both provisions require the processor to bind engaged sub-processors by contract or other legal act to the same data protection obligations / equivalent obligations. The contract clause expressly says the same data protection obligations as set out in the Agreement shall be imposed on the sub-processor.
results.condition_check: Compatible. The contract applies when the processor engages sub-processors for processing activities on behalf of the controller, which matches the regulation's engaged sub-processors context.
results.modality_check: Compatible. The regulation uses 'shall'; the contract also uses mandatory 'shall be imposed'.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible. The contract requires this by way of a contract or other legal act under EU or national Member State law, matching the regulation. The added reference to sufficient guarantees and technical/organisational measures is stricter, not looser.
results.contract_source_text: In the event that Processor engages Sub-processors for carrying out Processing activities on behalf of the Controller in accordance with Appendix 5, the same data protection obligations as set out in this Agreement shall be imposed on that Sub-processor by way of a contract or other legal act under EU or national Member State law, in particular providing sufficient guarantees to implement appropriate technical and organisational measures in such a manner that the Processing will meet the requirements of this Agreement.

results.core_alignment_check: Processor obligation to process personal data only on documented instructions from the controller matches the regulation's processor duty to process personal data on documented instructions from the controller.
results.condition_check: Compatible: both are limited to documented instructions from the controller; the contract is at least as strict by using 'only'.
results.modality_check: Equal-or-greater force: 'shall ... only' is mandatory and restrictive, satisfying the regulatory 'shall'.
results.other_constraints_check: No weaker constraints; the contract mirrors the same target and manner of processing on documented instructions.
results.contract_source_text: The Controller hereby authorises the Processor to Process the Personal Data on behalf of the Controller on the terms and conditions set out in this Agreement. The Processor shall Process the Personal Data only on documented instructions from the Controller. The Parties agree that this Agreement shall constitute the instructions as of the date of the Agreement. The Processor may – unless otherwise specifically is set out in the Agreement – apply all relevant means, including software, servers and IT systems.

results.core_alignment_check: Same obligation: processor processing personal data only on/in accordance with controller instructions, including transfer-related instructions, with a legal-compulsion exception and notice duty.
results.condition_check: Compatible: both are conditioned on controller instructions, with the contract adding the standard exception where Union/Member State law requires otherwise.
results.modality_check: Equal or stronger: 'shall process only in accordance with' is mandatory and restrictive, satisfying the regulatory 'shall process on documented instructions'.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: 'only in accordance with' is at least as strict as 'on documented instructions'; the added notice-before-processing requirement is extra and does not weaken the duty.
results.contract_source_text: to Process Personal Data only in accordance with the instructions from the Controller, cf. clause 2 of this Agreement, including with regard to transfers of Personal Data to a Third Country or an international organisation, unless required to do so by Union or Member State law to which the Processor is subject; in such a case, the Processor shall inform the Controller of that legal requirement before Processing, unless that law prohibits such information on important grounds of public interest;

results.core_alignment_check: Gap: the regulation requires the processor to process personal data only on documented instructions from the controller; the contract text instead speaks generally about ensuring collection complies with GDPR and applicable law, not about processing on documented controller instructions.
results.condition_check: No matching condition; the regulation's trigger is documented instructions from the controller, which is absent.
results.modality_check: No equivalent mandatory instruction-based processing obligation is stated.
results.other_constraints_check: The contract addresses lawful collection/compliance with data protection law, not the specific target/location/means constraint of documented instructions.
results.contract_source_text: To ensure that the Personal Data collected by Controller is done in accordance with the relevant provisions of the GDPR and other applicable EU and national data protection law in the Member State in which the Controller is established (and where applicable has been notified to the relevant authorities of that Member State) and does not violate the relevant provisions of that Member State. 5. General obligations of the Processor The Processor agrees:

results.core_alignment_check: Yes. The regulation requires the processor to ensure persons acting under its authority with access to personal data process only on the controller's instructions; the contract requires the processor to process personal data only on documented instructions from the controller, which is the same instruction-based restriction and is at least as strict.
results.condition_check: Compatible. Both apply to processing/access to personal data under the processor's authority; the contract does not add a conflicting condition and is narrower/stricter by requiring documented instructions.
results.modality_check: Compatible. The regulation uses a mandatory 'shall ensure'; the contract uses a mandatory 'shall process only on documented instructions', which is equal-or-greater force.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible. The contract's 'only on documented instructions' is at least as restrictive as 'only on instructions from the controller'; the additional statement that the agreement constitutes instructions does not weaken the duty.
results.contract_source_text: The Controller hereby authorises the Processor to Process the Personal Data on behalf of the Controller on the terms and conditions set out in this Agreement. The Processor shall Process the Personal Data only on documented instructions from the Controller. The Parties agree that this Agreement shall constitute the instructions as of the date of the Agreement. The Processor may – unless otherwise specifically is set out in the Agreement – apply all relevant means, including software, servers and IT systems.

results.core_alignment_check: Yes. Both impose a processor duty to make compliance-related information available to the controller (and here also the supervisory authority) for demonstrating compliance/audit purposes.
results.condition_check: Compatible. The contract adds 'on request' and includes the supervisory authority, which is not weaker than the regulation's availability duty.
results.modality_check: Compatible. 'Shall make available' matches the mandatory regulatory obligation.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible. 'Records' is an acceptable narrower-sounding vehicle for the information necessary to demonstrate compliance, and availability on request is consistent with the requirement.
results.contract_source_text: The Processor shall make the records available to the Controller and the supervisory authority on request. 8. Costs for assistance and audits

results.core_alignment_check: Yes. Both provisions impose a processor-side duty to implement security/protective measures for processing personal data; the contract’s technical and organisational measures to meet GDPR requirements and protect data subjects substantively cover the regulation’s requirement to take measures ensuring security of processing.
results.condition_check: Compatible. The regulation requires measures pursuant to Article 32 / to ensure security of processing; the contract requires measures implemented so processing meets GDPR and protects rights, which is at least as demanding and not narrower.
results.modality_check: Compatible. 'Agrees to implement' is an обязательство of equal force to 'shall take'.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible. The contract’s manner/target is broader but aligned: technical and organisational measures for processing personal data, aimed at GDPR compliance and protection of data subjects, which encompasses security of processing.
results.contract_source_text: The Processor agrees to implement appropriate technical and organisational measures in such a manner that the Processing of the Personal Data will meet the requirements of the GDPR and other applicable national and EU data protection law and ensure the protection of the rights of the Data Subjects.

results.core_alignment_check: Different assistance duty: the contract requires assisting the controller with DPIAs and prior consultation of supervisory authorities, not assisting the controller in notifying a personal data breach to the supervisory authority.
results.condition_check: Both are conditional assistance duties, but the triggering situations differ (DPIA/prior consultation vs personal data breach notification).
results.modality_check: Modality is comparable (processor shall assist), but it applies to a different task.
results.other_constraints_check: The contract adds 'where necessary' and 'taking into account the nature of the Processing,' which are compatible qualifiers, but they do not bridge the subject-matter mismatch.
results.contract_source_text: where necessary and taking into account the nature of the Processing, to assist the Controller in its compliance with an obligation to carry out a data protection impact assessment (“DPIA”) and prior consulting of supervisory authorities where required, cf. articles 35 and 36 of the GDPR;

results.core_alignment_check: Yes: the regulation requires the processor to take measures required by Article 32 to ensure processing security; the contract clause requires the Controller and Processor to implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure a level of security appropriate to the risk, including examples such as encryption and confidentiality/integrity/availability/resilience measures.
results.condition_check: Compatible: both are triggered by processing security/risk considerations under Article 32-style security obligations.
results.modality_check: Compatible: the contract uses 'shall implement' and thus imposes an equal-or-greater mandatory obligation.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: the contract's measures are at least as protective as the regulation's 'all measures required pursuant to Article 32' and directly address security of processing.
results.contract_source_text: Data relating to criminal convictions and offences (cf. GDPR art. 10). *Sensitive data includes: data revealing racial or ethnic origin, political opinions, religious and/or philosophical beliefs, trade union membership, processing of genetic data or biometric data for the purpose of uniquely identifying a natural person, data concerning health and/or data concerning a natural person's sex life or sexual orientation Please note that for the processing of sensitive data, explicit consent of the Data Subject is required. Appendix 2 Security of Processing This Appendix forms part of the Agreement. 1. Taking into account the state of the art, the costs of implementation and the nature, scope, context and purposes of Processing as well as the risk of varying likelihood and severity for the rights and freedoms of natural persons, the Controller and the Processor shall implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure a level of security appropriate to the risk, including inter alia as appropriate: (a) encryption of Personal Data when transmitted via public networks and in connection with remote access to Controller’s systems; (b) the ability to ensure the ongoing confidentiality, integrity, availability and resilience of Processing systems and Services;

results.core_alignment_check: Yes: the contract requires the controller and processor to implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure security, including encryption and ensuring confidentiality, integrity, availability and resilience of processing systems and services, which matches Art. 32(1)'s security-measures obligation.
results.condition_check: Compatible: both are conditioned on taking into account state of the art, costs, nature/scope/context/purposes of processing, and risk to rights and freedoms.
results.modality_check: Compatible: the contract uses 'shall implement' and 'shall' for the measures, which is at least as strong as the regulation's requirement.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: the contract includes equivalent security measures and the same risk-based standard; it is not looser on timing or scope for the measures it states.
results.contract_source_text: Data relating to criminal convictions and offences (cf. GDPR art. 10). *Sensitive data includes: data revealing racial or ethnic origin, political opinions, religious and/or philosophical beliefs, trade union membership, processing of genetic data or biometric data for the purpose of uniquely identifying a natural person, data concerning health and/or data concerning a natural person's sex life or sexual orientation Please note that for the processing of sensitive data, explicit consent of the Data Subject is required. Appendix 2 Security of Processing This Appendix forms part of the Agreement. 1. results.core_alignment_check: The contract clause is about processor assistance to the controller, but specifically for responding to data subject rights requests under Chapter III, not for ensuring the security of processing.
results.condition_check: The contract adds a compatible qualifier ('where necessary' and 'taking into account the nature of the Processing'), which is not weaker for the matched obligation.
results.modality_check: The clause imposes an assistance obligation ('to assist'), which is at least as strong as the regulation's 'shall assist'.
results.other_constraints_check: The target and object differ: the regulation requires assistance with security of processing, while the contract addresses assistance with data subject rights requests; these are different duties.
results.contract_source_text: where necessary and taking into account the nature of the Processing, to assist the Controller by appropriate technical and organisational measures, insofar as this is possible, for the fulfilment of the Controller's obligation by law to respond to requests for exercising the Data Subjects’ rights laid down in chapter III of the GDPR;

results.core_alignment_check: Both address processor liability for damages, but the regulation is a specific GDPR liability rule limited to damage caused by processing and only in two defined cases (non-compliance with processor-directed GDPR obligations or acting outside/contrary to lawful controller instructions), whereas the contract states a broader/general liability regime under Danish law subject to contractual limitations. The substantive obligation is not the same.
results.condition_check: The regulation has specific triggering conditions; the contract does not mirror those conditions and instead uses a general liability standard with clause-based limitations.
results.modality_check: Both are mandatory liability statements, but the contract does not impose the same specific liability rule required by the regulation.
results.other_constraints_check: The regulation is limited to damage caused by processing and to the two specified fault conditions; the contract is broader and different in scope and legal basis.
results.contract_source_text: The Processor shall be liable for damages in accordance with the general rules of Danish law subject to the limitations set out in this clause 13.

results.core_alignment_check: Different obligation: the regulation requires assisting the controller in communicating a personal data breach to the data subject, while the contract requires assisting with DPIAs and prior consultation of supervisory authorities.
results.condition_check: Not applicable / different triggering situation.
results.modality_check: Compatible for its own duty, but it is a different duty.
results.other_constraints_check: Different target and subject matter; no overlap with breach communication to data subjects.
results.contract_source_text: where necessary and taking into account the nature of the Processing, to assist the Controller in its compliance with an obligation to carry out a data protection impact assessment (“DPIA”) and prior consulting of supervisory authorities where required, cf. articles 35 and 36 of the GDPR;

shared_groups: Personal Data Breach Notification And Content
results.core_alignment_check: Yes: the regulation requires the breach notification to include the consequences of the breach, and the contract sub-item states 'the likely consequences of the Personal Data Breach' within a parent clause requiring the notification to describe the nature of the breach.
results.condition_check: Compatible: both concern the breach notification content; no additional limiting condition in the contract.
results.modality_check: Compatible: as a sub-item of a mandatory parent clause ('shall describe'), the descriptive statement is mandatory content.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: 'likely consequences' is substantively aligned with 'consequences of the breach' and is not weaker.
results.contract_source_text: the likely consequences of the Personal Data Breach;

results.core_alignment_check: Same processor-to-controller information duty: the regulation requires the processor to make available information necessary to demonstrate Article 28 compliance; the contract requires the processor, on request, to document sub-processor compliance by referring the controller to relevant audit reports or similar documentation made available by sub-processors, which is a narrower but substantively aligned form of making compliance information available.
results.condition_check: Compatible: both are triggered in the context of demonstrating compliance / on controller request for compliance documentation.
results.modality_check: Compatible and not weaker: the contract uses a mandatory entitlement structure for the processor to satisfy the request by providing the referenced documentation, which still results in information being made available; it is not a mere permission to withhold.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: the contract’s reference to audit reports or similar documentation, based on recognized audit standards, is an acceptable concrete vehicle for the required compliance information and does not make the duty looser.
results.contract_source_text: If the Controller requests the Processor to document its Sub-Processor’s compliance with the obligations laid down in Article 28 of the GDPR the Processor shall be entitled to fulfil this obligation by referring Controller to the relevant audit reports or similar documentation made available by the respective Sub-Processors with information indicating that the Sub-Processor in question complies with the GDPR, provided that such audit report(s) or similar documentation shall be based on applicable, acknowledged audit standards, e.g. ISAE 3000 or 3402, ISO 27001 or similar standards. Any additional audit reporting or additional other similar documentation requested by the Controller will be at the Controller’s cost and expenses and is charged under the conditions set out in Appendix 3. 11. Change of instructions

results.core_alignment_check: Processor informs Controller of intended changes to add/replace sub-processors and gives Controller opportunity/right to object; same obligation as controller's right to object after being informed.
results.condition_check: Same triggering situation: general written authorisation and intended changes concerning addition/replacement of sub-processors.
results.modality_check: Equal or stronger: contract gives opportunity/right to object and restricts objection only in a specified case, which does not weaken the basic right.
results.other_constraints_check: Timing/sequence compatible: objection follows processor notification; 30-day deemed acceptance is an added procedural constraint but does not negate the initial right to object.
results.contract_source_text: In the case of general written authorisation, the Processor shall inform the Controller of any intended changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors, thereby giving the Controller the opportunity to object to such changes. However, the Controller cannot object to any intended changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors, if the new Sub-processor provides sufficient guarantees with respect to implementation of appropriate technical and organizational measures in such a manner that the Processing will meet the requirements of the GDPR as outlined in this Agreement. If the Controller does not object to changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors within 30 days from the Processor’s notification of the intended changes, such changes shall be deemed to be accepted by the Controller.

results.core_alignment_check: The regulation requires the processor to make available to the controller information necessary to demonstrate compliance with Article 28 obligations. The contract instead addresses documenting a sub-processor’s compliance and allows referral to audit reports or similar documentation from sub-processors; this is about sub-processor audit evidence, not the processor making available to the controller the information necessary to demonstrate the processor’s own Article 28 compliance.
results.condition_check: The regulation is unconditional; the contract only applies if the Controller requests documentation of sub-processor compliance.
results.modality_check: The contract is permissive/entitlement-based ('shall be entitled to fulfil this obligation by referring...') rather than a direct obligation to make available the required information.
results.other_constraints_check: The contract is narrower in object and recipient: it concerns sub-processor audit reports and additional audit reporting at the controller's cost, not the processor's general duty to provide compliance information.
results.contract_source_text: If the Controller requests the Processor to document its Sub-Processor’s compliance with the obligations laid down in Article 28 of the GDPR the Processor shall be entitled to fulfil this obligation by referring Controller to the relevant audit reports or similar documentation made available by the respective Sub-Processors with information indicating that the Sub-Processor in question complies with the GDPR, provided that such audit report(s) or similar documentation shall be based on applicable, acknowledged audit standards, e.g. ISAE 3000 or 3402, ISO 27001 or similar standards. Any additional audit reporting or additional other similar documentation requested by the Controller will be at the Controller’s cost and expenses and is charged under the conditions set out in Appendix 3. 11. results.modality_check: Compatible on the covered item: 'shall describe' is mandatory and at least as strong as the regulation for the nature-of-breach element.

results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: contract adds an extra notice duty to inform the controller before processing unless prohibited, which is stricter and does not weaken the exception.
results.contract_source_text: to Process Personal Data only in accordance with the instructions from the Controller, cf. clause 2 of this Agreement, including with regard to transfers of Personal Data to a Third Country or an international organisation, unless required to do so by Union or Member State law to which the Processor is subject; in such a case, the Processor shall inform the Controller of that legal requirement before Processing, unless that law prohibits such information on important grounds of public interest;

results.core_alignment_check: Yes: both address the processor's obligation to regularly test, assess, and evaluate the effectiveness of technical and organisational security measures for processing.

results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: the subject matter, security focus, and scope of measures align; references to hosting, risks, and external security descriptions do not weaken the core obligation.

results.core_alignment_check: Yes. Both provisions address processor liability for damage/loss in connection with processing and allocate liability based on fault/causation. The regulation makes the processor liable only when it has breached processor-directed GDPR obligations or acted outside/contrary to lawful controller instructions. The contract instead states the processor is not liable when damage is caused by the controller’s own failures, breaches of the main agreement, or failures toward a supervisory authority. This is related to liability allocation, but it does not cover the same substantive trigger conditions for processor liability under Art. 82(2).
results.condition_check: Not aligned. The regulation’s conditions are processor non-compliance with processor-specific GDPR duties or acting contrary to lawful controller instructions. The contract conditions are controller breaches and supervisory-authority-related failures, which are different causal triggers.
results.modality_check: The contract uses a non-liability exclusion, which is not weaker in force by itself, but it applies to different circumstances than the regulation.
results.other_constraints_check: The regulation is limited to damage caused by processing and to processor misconduct; the contract extends non-liability to controller breaches and penalties. That is a different scope and does not match the regulation’s target conditions.
results.contract_source_text: The Processor shall not be liable for loss or damages if caused by the Controller’s failure to comply with its obligations according to applicable EU or national data protection laws and regulations or this Agreement. Nor shall the Processor be liable for loss or damages in the event of the Controller’s breach of the Main Agreement and/or other agreement between the Parties involving Processing of Personal Data, or the Controller’s failure to comply with its obligations towards a supervisory authority, or for penalties imposed by a supervisory authority due to the Controller’s breach or failure to comply.

results.contract_source_text: The Controller hereby authorises the Processor to Process the Personal Data on behalf of the Controller on the terms and conditions set out in this Agreement. The Processor shall Process the Personal Data only on documented instructions from the Controller. The Parties agree that this Agreement shall constitute the instructions as of the date of the Agreement. The Processor may – unless otherwise specifically is set out in the Agreement – apply all relevant means, including software, servers and IT systems.

results.core_alignment_check: No. The regulation requires the processor to impose Article 28(3)-equivalent obligations on engaged sub-processors by contract or other legal act; the contract clause instead says the processor remains liable to the controller for the sub-processor's performance, which is a liability allocation, not an obligation-imposition duty.
results.condition_check: Compatible in that both concern sub-processors, but the contract does not address the same triggering duty.
results.modality_check: The contract uses 'shall', but it is directed to liability rather than imposing contractual obligations on sub-processors.
results.other_constraints_check: The regulation requires action by way of contract or other legal act under EU/Member State law; the contract clause contains no such requirement and instead addresses liability only.
results.contract_source_text: Processor shall remain liable to the Controller for the performance of its Sub- processor's obligations.

results.core_alignment_check: Yes: both require the processor to process personal data only/on the basis of documented instructions from the controller.
results.condition_check: Compatible: both are limited to documented instructions from the controller; the contract is at least as strict by saying only on such instructions.
results.modality_check: Compatible: the contract uses 'shall ... only', which is mandatory and restrictive, meeting the regulation's 'shall'.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: 'on behalf of the Controller' and 'only on documented instructions' match the required manner/target of processing.
results.contract_source_text: The Controller hereby authorises the Processor to Process the Personal Data on behalf of the Controller on the terms and conditions set out in this Agreement. Appendix 2 Security of Processing This Appendix forms part of the Agreement. 1. Taking into account the state of the art, the costs of implementation and the nature, scope, context and purposes of Processing as well as the risk of varying likelihood and severity for the rights and freedoms of natural persons, the Controller and the Processor shall implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure a level of security appropriate to the risk, including inter alia as appropriate: (a) encryption of Personal Data when transmitted via public networks and in connection with remote access to Controller’s systems; (b) the ability to ensure the ongoing confidentiality, integrity, availability and resilience of Processing systems and Services;

results.modality_check: Obligation in contract, but on a different obligation.

results.core_alignment_check: No: the regulation requires the processor to assist the controller in responding to data subject rights requests, while the contract clause is about documenting sub-processor compliance with Article 28 via audit reports/documentation.
results.condition_check: Different trigger and subject matter: controller requests documentation of sub-processor compliance, not requests to exercise data subject rights.
results.modality_check: The contract uses a permissive/entitlement framing for a different task, not an obligation to assist with rights requests.
results.other_constraints_check: The clause adds audit-report standards and cost allocation, which are unrelated to the regulation's assistance duty.
results.contract_source_text: If the Controller requests the Processor to document its Sub-Processor’s compliance with the obligations laid down in Article 28 of the GDPR the Processor shall be entitled to fulfil this obligation by referring Controller to the relevant audit reports or similar documentation made available by the respective Sub-Processors with information indicating that the Sub-Processor in question complies with the GDPR, provided that such audit report(s) or similar documentation shall be based on applicable, acknowledged audit standards, e.g. ISAE 3000 or 3402, ISO 27001 or similar standards. Any additional audit reporting or additional other similar documentation requested by the Controller will be at the Controller’s cost and expenses and is charged under the conditions set out in Appendix 3. 11. results.core_alignment_check: Yes. Both clauses impose a processor duty to assist the controller with the controller's legal obligation to respond to data subject rights requests under GDPR Chapter III.
results.condition_check: Compatible. The contract adds 'where necessary' and 'insofar as this is possible,' which are reasonable qualifiers and not weaker than the regulation's general assistance duty.
results.modality_check: Compatible. 'Shall assist' is mandatory and matches the regulation's obligation.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible. The contract specifies the manner of assistance ('appropriate technical and organisational measures') and the same target obligation (responding to requests for exercising data subject rights).
results.contract_source_text: where necessary and taking into account the nature of the Processing, to assist the Controller by appropriate technical and organisational measures, insofar as this is possible, for the fulfilment of the Controller's obligation by law to respond to requests for exercising the Data Subjects’ rights laid down in chapter III of the GDPR;

results.core_alignment_check: The regulation requires that, under a general written authorization for sub-processors, the controller has a right to object to intended changes involving addition or replacement of sub-processors after being informed by the processor. The contract gives the controller prior general written authorization for the processor to use sub-processors and lists current sub-processors, but it does not state any right for the controller to object to additions/replacements or any notice-and-objection mechanism for changes.
results.condition_check: The regulation is triggered by intended changes to the addition or replacement of sub-processors after information is given. The contract instead grants a general authorization to use sub-processors and lists existing ones; it does not address change notifications or objections.
results.modality_check: The regulation requires a controller right to object. The contract provides authorization to the processor to use sub-processors, which is not the same obligation and does not include the required objection right.
results.other_constraints_check: The relevant timing/sequence is notice of intended changes followed by an opportunity to object. The contract only makes a list of sub-processors available and does not include the change-related timing or objection process.

results.core_alignment_check: The regulation requires the controller to have a right to terminate the DPA in certain cases. The contract gives each party, including the controller, an entitlement to terminate the agreement with 60 days' written notice if the parties cannot agree on reasonable terms for implementing necessary GDPR/data-protection-law changes. This is the same real-world termination right, and it is at least as broad as the requirement.
results.condition_check: Compatible: both are conditional termination rights in specified cases; the contract's condition (failure to agree on reasonable terms for necessary legal/GDPR changes) fits within the regulation's 'certain cases' and is not weaker.
results.modality_check: Compatible: 'shall be entitled to terminate' grants a right, which meets the requirement that the controller shall have the right to terminate.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: the contract adds a 60-day written notice requirement, which is a permissible additional constraint and does not make the right weaker in substance.
results.contract_source_text: The Processor shall use reasonable endeavours to comply with any legislative changes. However, the Processor shall not be obligated to implement any change of the instructions if the Parties cannot in good faith agree to reasonable terms for the implementation. If the Parties fail to agree in good faith to reasonable terms regarding change of the instructions each Party shall be entitled to terminate this Agreement with a written notice of 60 days, provided that such changes are deemed necessary to comply with the GDPR or other applicable EU or national data protection laws and regulation. The Main Agreement and any other agreement between the Parties involving Processing of Personal Data shall automatically terminate at the same time.

results.core_alignment_check: Yes. The regulation requires the processor to take measures to ensure security of processing under Article 32, and the contract clause requires testing, assessing and evaluating the effectiveness of technical and organisational measures for ensuring security, identifies security risks to personal data, and states that the processor provides/implements security measures. This is the same real-world security obligation, and the contract is at least as specific/strict.
results.condition_check: Compatible. Both are triggered by the need to ensure security of processing; the contract’s references to assessing appropriate security level and risks are consistent with Article 32’s risk-based security duty.
results.modality_check: Compatible. The regulation imposes a shall-duty, and the contract uses mandatory language ('shall be taken', 'are specified', 'provides'), which is equal or stronger.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible. The contract’s focus on technical and organisational measures, risks from accidental/unlawful destruction, loss, alteration, unauthorised disclosure, or access, and security of personal data aligns with the regulation’s security-of-processing requirement and is not looser.
results.contract_source_text: a process for testing, assessing and evaluating the effectiveness of technical and organisational measures for ensuring the security of the Processing. 2. In assessing the appropriate level of security account shall be taken in particular of the risks that are presented by Processing, in particular from accidental or unlawful destruction, loss, alteration, unauthorised disclosure of, or access to Personal Data transmitted, stored or otherwise processed. 3. The Processor hosts the data, including Personal Data, of the Controller via Microsoft Cloud Azure. The online hosting Services are delivered from data centres solely situated within EU, in Ireland and the Netherlands. 4. Microsoft Azure platform provides the security measures as described on www.Processor.com. 5. Particular security measures to be taken out by the Processor under this Agreement are specified in detail on www.Processor.com (Processor's Security and Privacy Protection Policies). results.contract_source_text: In the case of general written authorisation, the Processor shall inform the Controller of any intended changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors, thereby giving the Controller the opportunity to object to such changes. However, the Controller cannot object to any intended changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors, if the new Sub-processor provides sufficient guarantees with respect to implementation of appropriate technical and organizational measures in such a manner that the Processing will meet the requirements of the GDPR as outlined in this Agreement. If the Controller does not object to changes concerning the addition or replacement of Sub-processors within 30 days from the Processor’s notification of the intended changes, such changes shall be deemed to be accepted by the Controller.

results.condition_check: The regulation is triggered by the existence of a legal requirement and the absence of a public-interest prohibition on informing the controller; the contract is triggered by agreed changes to instructions, which is a different situation.
results.modality_check: The contract uses mandatory 'shall', but it mandates a different action, not the required notification.
results.other_constraints_check: Timing and manner differ: 'before processing' and the public-interest qualifier are absent; 'without undue delay' relates to implementing changes, not informing of legal requirements.
results.contract_source_text: The Processor shall without undue delay initiate implementation of agreed changes of the instructions and shall ensure that such changes are implemented without undue delay in relation to the nature and extent of the changes.

results.modality_check: The contract uses mandatory language for the deletion/export assistance it does cover, but that obligation is about a different task and does not extend to DPIA assistance.
results.other_constraints_check: The timing and payment constraints concern deletion/export assistance and are irrelevant to the DPIA assistance duty.

results.modality_check: The regulation imposes an immediate duty to inform. The contract text is a general compliance/ensuring statement, not an equivalent notification obligation.

results.core_alignment_check: Gap: the regulation requires the processor to take into account specific security risks when assessing security, while the contract merely points to details of security measures in Appendix 2 rather than stating the risk-assessment obligation itself.

results.contract_source_text: The details of the security measures taken by Processor in this respect of the Processing of Personal Data, are specified in Appendix 2, which forms an integral part of the Agreement.

results.core_alignment_check: The regulation requires the processor to take the risk of accidental or unlawful destruction, loss, alteration, unauthorized disclosure of, or access to personal data into account when assessing security. The contract parent clause requires the Controller and Processor to implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure a level of security appropriate to the risk, explicitly taking into account the state of the art, costs, nature/scope/context/purposes of processing, and the risk of varying likelihood and severity for rights and freedoms. This is the same security-risk assessment/mitigation obligation and is at least as protective.
results.condition_check: Compatible: both are triggered in the context of assessing/ensuring security for processing personal data and both hinge on risk considerations.
results.modality_check: Compatible: the contract uses mandatory 'shall implement' language, which is equal or stronger than the regulation's 'shall take into account'.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: the contract is stricter, requiring appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure security appropriate to the risk, which exceeds merely taking the specified risks into account.
results.contract_source_text: Data relating to criminal convictions and offences (cf. GDPR art. 10). *Sensitive data includes: data revealing racial or ethnic origin, political opinions, religious and/or philosophical beliefs, trade union membership, processing of genetic data or biometric data for the purpose of uniquely identifying a natural person, data concerning health and/or data concerning a natural person's sex life or sexual orientation Please note that for the processing of sensitive data, explicit consent of the Data Subject is required. Appendix 2 Security of Processing This Appendix forms part of the Agreement. 1. Taking into account the state of the art, the costs of implementation and the nature, scope, context and purposes of Processing as well as the risk of varying likelihood and severity for the rights and freedoms of natural persons, the Controller and the Processor shall implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure a level of security appropriate to the risk, including inter alia as appropriate: (a) encryption of Personal Data when transmitted via public networks and in connection with remote access to Controller’s systems; (b) the ability to ensure the ongoing confidentiality, integrity, availability and resilience of Processing systems and Services;

results.core_alignment_check: Same processor duty to notify/inform the controller, but the trigger is different: contract covers processing contrary to instructions / legal requirement before processing, not specifically an instruction that infringes GDPR or other data protection provisions.

results.other_constraints_check: Timing differs but is not weaker for its own trigger ('before Processing' vs 'immediately'); however it does not cover the regulation's infringement-triggered notice obligation.

results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: the object and purpose match security of processing; no conflicting timing, target, or location constraints appear for this obligation.
